vulnerability_chains.title: Insecure Deserialization to RCE
vulnerability_chains.description: Deserialization of untrusted data can lead to arbitrary code execution through gadget chains
vulnerability_chains.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker crafts malicious serialized payload with gadget chain
2. Payload is sent to deserialization point at line 76 in /home/mundur/Documents/pentest/Chia Network/chia-blockchain/chia/_tests/util/blockchain.py
3. Gadget chain triggers arbitrary code execution
4. Attacker gains full server control

steps.description: Gadget chain leads to code execution

vulnerability_chains.impact: Complete server compromise via arbitrary code execution

vulnerability_chains.title: SSRF to Internal Service/Data Access
vulnerability_chains.description: Server-side request forgery can be leveraged to access internal services, cloud metadata, or local files
vulnerability_chains.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker exploits SSRF at line 50 in /home/mundur/Documents/pentest/Chia Network/chia-blockchain/chia/wallet/util/peer_request_cache.py
2. Crafts request to http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ (AWS metadata)
3. Retrieves IAM credentials or internal service tokens
4. Pivots to internal network or escalates privileges

steps.description: SSRF allows attacker to make server-side requests

vulnerability_chains.impact: Access to cloud metadata, internal services, credential theft

vulnerability_chains.title: Path Traversal → Sensitive File Read → Credential Theft
vulnerability_chains.description: Directory traversal allows reading arbitrary files including secrets and credentials
vulnerability_chains.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker exploits path traversal at line 150 in /home/mundur/Documents/pentest/Chia Network/chia-blockchain/chia/data_layer/s3_plugin_service.py
2. Reads /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, or .env files
3. Extracts database credentials or API keys
4. Pivots to database or external service access
vulnerability_chains.impact: Credential theft, lateral movement, data breach

vulnerability_chains.title: Path Traversal → Sensitive File Read → Credential Theft
vulnerability_chains.description: Directory traversal allows reading arbitrary files including secrets and credentials
vulnerability_chains.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker exploits path traversal at line 26 in /home/mundur/Documents/pentest/Chia Network/chia-blockchain/chia/util/config.py
2. Reads /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, or .env files
3. Extracts database credentials or API keys
4. Pivots to database or external service access
vulnerability_chains.impact: Credential theft, lateral movement, data breach
